etymology.description: semantically: 隹(隹) (zhuī, "bird") + abbreviated phonetic: 暵(暵) (xiàn, "dry") -> semantically refers to a type of bird, later phonetically borrowed for "difficult"
etymology.interpretation: The character 难(難) (nán, "difficult") combines the semantic component 隹(隹) (zhuī, "bird"), originally depicting a type of bird, with the phonetic component 暵(暵) (xiàn, "dry"). It was later phonetically borrowed to represent 'difficult.'
etymology.simplification: 难(難): The traditional component 隹(隹) (zhuī, "bird") was replaced by 又(又) (yòu, "again"), simplifying the structure while retaining the phonetic.

examples.chinese: 学习新的语言对我来说很难(學習新的語言對我來說很難)。
examples.pinyin: Xuéxí xīn de yǔyán duì wǒ lái shuō hěn nán.
examples.english: Learning a new language is difficult for me.

examples.chinese: 这个数学题很难(這個數學題很難)，我花了两个小时才解出来(我花了兩個小時才解出來)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège shùxuétí hěn nán, wǒ huāle liǎng gè xiǎoshí cái jiě chūlái.
examples.english: This math problem is difficult; it took me two hours to solve it.

examples.chinese: 虽然任务很难(雖然任務很難)，但我们一定能完成(但我們一定能完成)。
examples.pinyin: Suīrán rènwù hěn nán, dàn wǒmen yīdìng néng wánchéng.
examples.english: Although the task is difficult, we will definitely complete it.
